code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace, preserving useful parenthetical notes."""
        cleaned = value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        # Keep parenthetical information (e.g., "(defunct record label)") because it may be
        # relevant for the generated description.
        return cleaned

    def _format_subject(subj: str) -> str:
        """If subject contains a role in parentheses, move the role before the name."""
        m = re.match(r'^(.*?)\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*$', subj)
        if m:
            name = m.group(1).strip()
            role = m.group(2).strip()
            # Capitalize role if needed
            role = role.lower()
            if not role.startswith("the "):
                role = "the " + role
            return f"{role} {name}"
        return subj

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "genre": "is a {obj} artist",
        "instrument": "plays {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is associated with {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "record label is {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started performing in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended performing in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "has its stylistic origins in {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "is fused with {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "is a subgenre of {obj}",
        "derivative": "has a derivative {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        # leader is handled specially to place the object as the subject.
        "leader": "has leader {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "belongs to the country {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "is a {obj}",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate) to allow proper pluralisation.
    from collections import defaultdict

    # Helper to format a list of objects in natural language.
    def _fmt_list(items: list[str]) -> str:
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        if len(items) == 2:
            return f"{items[0]} and {items[1]}"
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + ", and " + items[-1]

    # Templates for singular and plural forms.
    singular_map = pred_map.copy()
    plural_map = {
        "recordLabel": "record labels are {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is associated with {obj}",
        "genre": "genres are {obj}",
        "instrument": "plays {obj}",
        "occupation": "occupations are {obj}",
        "nationality": "nationalities are {obj}",
        "background": "has background {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        # default fallback for any predicate with multiple values
    }

    # Collect objects per (subject, predicate)
    subj_pred_objs: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        subj_pred_objs[(subj, pred)].append(obj)

    # Build a map of genre -> its stylistic origin (to embed later)
    genre_origin: dict[str, str] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate == "stylisticOrigin":
            # Subject is the genre, object is the origin
            genre = _clean(t.subject)
            origin = _clean(t.object)
            genre_origin[genre] = origin

    # Helper to add appropriate article for certain instruments
    def _article(item: str) -> str:
        lower = item.lower()
        need_article = {
            "piano", "guitar", "bass", "drums", "violin",
            "trumpet", "saxophone", "flute", "cello",
            "harp", "keyboard", "organ"
        }
        return f"the {item}" if lower in need_article else item

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Build sentences, handling special cases (genre + instrument, derivatives)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # First, process derivative triples separately, producing sentences like
    # "Funk is derived from Jazz."
    derivative_sentences: list[str] = []
    for (subj, pred), objs in list(subj_pred_objs.items()):
        if pred != "derivative":
            continue
        if len(objs) == 1:
            derivative_sentences.append(f"{objs[0]} is derived from {subj}")
        else:
            derivative_sentences.append(f"{_fmt_list(objs)} are derived from {subj}")
        # Remove so they are not processed again
        del subj_pred_objs[(subj, pred)]

    # Group remaining predicates by subject
    subject_data: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for (subj, pred), objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        subject_data[subj][pred].extend(objs)

    # Preferred order for predicates when constructing the main clause
    pred_order = [
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist",
        "genre",
        "instrument",
        "occupation",
        "origin",
        "birthPlace",
        "birthDate",
        "birthYear",
        "activeYearsStartYear",
        "activeYearsEndYear",
        "recordLabel",
        "nationality",
        "background",
    ]

    sentences: list[str] = derivative_sentences  # start with derivative sentences

    for subj, pred_dict in subject_data.items():
        # Reformat subject if it contains a role in parentheses
        subj = _format_subject(subj)
        parts: list[str] = []

        # 1️⃣ Genre – create “is a X genre musician” phrase
        if "genre" in pred_dict:
            genre_str = _fmt_list(pred_dict["genre"])
            parts.append(f"is a {genre_str} musician")

        # 2️⃣ Instrument – create “plays the X and Y” phrase
        if "instrument" in pred_dict:
            instrs = [_article(o) for o in pred_dict["instrument"]]
            instr_str = _fmt_list(instrs)
            parts.append(f"plays {instr_str}")

        # 3️⃣ Other predicates – use existing mapping tables
        for pred in pred_order:
            if pred in ("genre", "instrument"):
                continue
            if pred not in pred_dict:
                continue
            objs = pred_dict[pred]
            objs_str = _fmt_list(objs)

            # Choose singular or plural template
            if len(objs) > 1 and pred in plural_map:
                template = plural_map[pred]
            else:
                template = singular_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {objs_str}")

            clause = template.format(obj=objs_str) if "{obj}" in template else template
            parts.append(clause)

        # 4️⃣ Any remaining predicates not in pred_order
        remaining_preds = [p for p in pred_dict.keys() if p not in pred_order]
        for pred in remaining_preds:
            objs = pred_dict[pred]
            objs_str = _fmt_list(objs)
            if len(objs) > 1 and pred in plural_map:
                template = plural_map[pred]
            else:
                template = singular_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {objs_str}")
            clause = template.format(obj=objs_str) if "{obj}" in template else template
            parts.append(clause)

        # Assemble the final sentence for this subject
        if parts:
            body = ", ".join(parts)
            sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
            sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
